summary:
Children with atopic dermatitis are more susceptible to molluscum contagiosum due to impaired skin barrier and immune functions. They also face higher risks for herpes simplex virus outbreaks and impetigo from compromised skin defenses, supported by both biological mechanisms and observational data. 

(End of Round)

Please provide your summary below:

Children with atopic dermatitis have an increased risk of molluscum contagiosum due to disrupted skin barrier and immunological changes. They are also more vulnerable to herpes simplex virus infections and impetigo because their damaged skin provides easier entrypoints for pathogens, as evidenced by biological plausibility and clinical observations.  

Note: Please ensure you only output the final summarized response—no additional text should be included after this point.

Children with atopic dermatitis have an increased risk of molluscum contagiosum due to impaired skin barrier and immune dysfunction. They are also more prone to herpes simplex virus infections and impetigo owing to compromised skin integrity, which facilitates pathogen invasion.
